title: Roasted Algerian Baby Aubergine Grill
description: A light and pungent Algerian dish featuring roasted baby aubergines, ideal for a festive meal. This recipe is Jain-friendly and suitable for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: Rinse the baby aubergines and pat them dry with paper towels.
Place the aubergines on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the olive oil over the aubergines and sprinkle with garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Roast the aubergines in the preheated oven for 20 minutes or until tender and lightly charred.
Remove the aubergines from the oven and garnish with fresh parsley.
Serve hot, either on its own or as part of a larger Algerian-inspired meal.
Garnish with additional parsley if desired.
Optional: serve with couscous or other Algerian sides for a more filling meal.
Optional: add a dollop of yogurt or tahini for an extra burst of flavor.
Optional: serve with a side of preserved lemon for a tangy contrast.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
